sourceTitle: Sprints, simplified with Notion’s sprint tracking system

questions.question: What is the first step to add sprint functionality to a brand‑new Notion workspace?

options.text: Open the Tasks database and toggle the Sprints option.
options.explanation: This option only works if you already have a Tasks database; a new workspace starts with no databases.

options.text: Go to the Templates menu in the sidebar and add the “Projects, tasks, & sprints” template.
options.explanation: The guide says to select this template when starting from scratch, which creates the needed databases including Sprints.

options.text: Create a new page named “Sprints” and manually link it to existing tasks.
options.explanation: Manually creating a page does not set up the relational databases and board view that the template provides.

options.text: Enable the “Automated sprints” toggle in the Options menu.
options.explanation: Automation can only be turned on after the Sprints database exists; it’s not the initial step.

questions.question: When you turn on sprints for an existing Tasks database, what new element is automatically created?

options.text: A third database called Sprints linked by a relation property.
options.explanation: The guide explains that enabling sprints creates a Sprints database linked to Tasks.

options.text: A new column called "Sprint Owner" inside the Tasks database.
options.explanation: The feature adds a separate Sprints database, not a column named Sprint Owner.

options.text: A Kanban board within the Projects database.
options.explanation: The Kanban board appears in the Sprints database, not the Projects database.

options.text: A Slack channel automatically generated for sprint notifications.
options.explanation: Slack notifications require a separate automation; they are not created automatically.

questions.question: During a sprint, where do team members typically go to update the status of their tasks?

options.text: The Current view of the Sprint board, which uses a Kanban layout.
options.explanation: The Current view shows tasks by status in a Kanban board, making it the place to move cards.

options.text: The Backlog view of the Tasks database.
options.explanation: Backlog contains unassigned tasks; status updates happen on the sprint board.

options.text: The Sprint planning view before the sprint starts.
options.explanation: Planning view is for initial assignment, not for ongoing status changes.

options.text: The Project’s timeline view.
options.explanation: Timeline view shows sprint dates, not individual task status.

questions.question: What happens when you click “Complete Sprint 1” in the sprint board?

options.text: Sprint 1 is archived, Sprint 2 becomes the Current sprint, and you can choose where to move incomplete tasks.
options.explanation: The guide states that completing a sprint marks it as Past, sets the next sprint as Current, and offers options for unfinished tasks.

options.text: All tasks from Sprint 1 are automatically deleted.
options.explanation: Tasks are not deleted; you decide whether to move them to backlog, next sprint, or keep them.

options.text: The sprint’s start and end dates become locked and cannot be edited.
options.explanation: You can still adjust dates for the next sprint in the completion dialog.

options.text: The sprint board switches to a timeline view automatically.
options.explanation: Completing a sprint does not change the view mode; you must select the view manually.

questions.question: If you need to add a new task to an ongoing sprint, which action should you take?

options.text: Click the “+” button in the New category on the Sprint board and assign an owner.
options.explanation: The guide explains that new tasks can be added directly from the Sprint board using the + in the New category.

options.text: Create the task in the Projects database and then manually link it to the sprint.
options.explanation: While possible, the quickest method during a sprint is adding it from the Sprint board.

options.text: Add the task to the Backlog view and wait for the next sprint planning session.
options.explanation: Backlog is for unassigned tasks; you can add directly to the current sprint instead.

options.text: Send a Slack message to the team asking them to create the task.
options.explanation: Slack notifications are optional automations, not the prescribed way to add tasks.

questions.question: Which feature lets you automatically create the next sprint and close the current one without manual steps?

options.text: Turn on “Automated sprints” in the Sprints options menu.
options.explanation: The guide highlights the Automated sprints toggle as a way to cut out manual completion and creation.

options.text: Enable the “Progress ring” view in the Sprints database.
options.explanation: Progress ring visualizes completion but does not automate sprint creation.

options.text: Set a recurring reminder in the calendar integration.
options.explanation: Calendar reminders are unrelated to the built‑in sprint automation feature.

options.text: Create a Zapier workflow that duplicates the sprint template.
options.explanation: Zapier can be used, but the native Automated sprints toggle is the feature described.

questions.question: How can you view the proportion of tasks completed in a sprint?

options.text: Look at the progress ring displayed in the Sprints database.
options.explanation: The guide mentions a progress ring that shows the percentage of completed tasks per sprint.

options.text: Check the number of cards in the ‘Done’ column of the Kanban board.
options.explanation: While informative, the guide specifies the progress ring as the visual metric.

options.text: Read the sprint’s summary text field in the Planning notes toggle.
options.explanation: Planning notes are for goals, not automatic completion metrics.

options.text: Open the Slack channel to see the notification count.
options.explanation: Slack notifications are optional and do not display completion percentages.

questions.question: What advantage does linking tasks to projects via a relation property provide?

options.text: It lets you quickly see the larger initiative each task belongs to and access related context with a few clicks.
options.explanation: The guide emphasizes that relation links give immediate visibility into the broader project context.

options.text: It automatically moves completed tasks to the backlog.
options.explanation: Relation links do not dictate task movement; that is handled by sprint status changes.

options.text: It generates a Gantt chart for each sprint.
options.explanation: Gantt charts are not mentioned; the timeline view shows sprint overlap, not per‑task Gantt charts.

options.text: It disables the ability to edit task details while the sprint is active.
options.explanation: Relation properties do not restrict editing; they simply connect records.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a customization option available in the sprint options menu?

options.text: Choosing the default duration of sprints.
options.explanation: The guide states you can set the default sprint length in the options menu.

options.text: Selecting which day of the week new sprints should begin.
options.explanation: The guide mentions you can choose the start day for new sprints.

options.text: Enabling automated sprint completion and creation.
options.explanation: Automation can be toggled in the options menu.

options.text: Setting a budget limit for each sprint.
options.explanation: Budget limits are not discussed as a sprint option; the menu focuses on timing and automation.

questions.question: If a team wants to receive a Slack notification every time a sprint finishes, how should they implement this?

options.text: Create a database automation in the Sprints database that sends a Slack message on the sprint completion trigger.
options.explanation: The guide describes using database automations to send Slack notifications upon sprint completion.

options.text: Enable the built‑in Slack integration toggle in the sprint options menu.
options.explanation: There is no direct toggle; notifications require setting up an automation.

options.text: Add a Slack webhook URL to each task’s comment section.
options.explanation: Webhooks in task comments would not automatically fire on sprint completion.

options.text: Configure a recurring calendar event that posts to Slack.
options.explanation: Calendar events are unrelated to sprint completion automations.
